seo.title: Falls Prevention Platform for Operators & Clinicians | Able Care
seo.description: Objective screening, risk stratification and clinical proof in one workflow. Roll it out across every branch, community or clinical site.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Patient performing standing exercise during assessment
blocks.headline: The objective case for prevention.
blocks.subtitle: Objective screening, risk stratification, and clinical proof in one workflow. Roll it out across every branch, community or clinical site.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Upper-body strength proxy. Predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb power and balance. A key indicator of fall likelihood.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to mortality, hospitalization and functional decline.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whatItMeasures: Falls risk in under a minute. One of the most widely validated functional assessments.

blocks.heading: Backed by the research
blocks.pulledStat: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

citations.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the PURE study
Multifactorial falls risk assessment outperforms single-test approaches
Objective screening reduces falls by 25% in community-dwelling older adults

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: A five-minute assessment on any tablet. Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go captured in one workflow.

steps.title: Score
steps.description: Instant risk banding against normative data. No subjective scoring. No interpretation required.

steps.title: Act
steps.description: Care plan triggers routed to the right team. Risk stratification that operators can standardize across every site.

steps.title: Track
steps.description: Longitudinal trends surface decline before it becomes a fall. Population-level insight for operators.

blocks.heading: We sit upstream of detection and response
blocks.imageAlt: Diagram showing Able Care's position upstream of fall detection and response
blocks.description: Fall detection looks at falls after they happen. Balance training apps push exercises to residents. Paper assessments ask clinicians to score by observation. Able Care screens every resident or client objectively, stratifies risk, and hands the operator a standardized workflow they can roll out to every site.

blocks.heading: Able Assess vs paper-based assessment
blocks.tldr: Paper assessments take 20+ minutes, are scored subjectively, and rarely make it into the care plan. Able Assess runs in under 5, scores automatically, and routes results to the team.

caseStudies.title: Home Care
caseStudies.summary: Detected functional decline six weeks earlier than the previous paper-based process.

caseStudies.title: Senior Living
caseStudies.summary: Reduced falls-related resident turnover and protected occupancy rates across the community.

caseStudies.title: Clinical
caseStudies.summary: Standardized multifactorial screening across a multi-site clinical network.

stats.label: Reduction in falls with objective screening

faqs.question: How is Able Care different from SafelyYou?
faqs.answer: SafelyYou detects falls after they happen. Able Care screens residents before they fall. We sit upstream of detection and response, at the prevention layer.

faqs.question: How is Able Care different from balance training apps like Nymbl?
faqs.answer: Balance training apps push exercises to residents. Able Assess screens every resident objectively and stratifies risk so operators know where to focus prevention resources.

faqs.question: Can non-clinical staff run the screening?
faqs.answer: Yes. Able Assess was built for non-clinical staff. Five minutes per resident. No training course required.

faqs.question: Where is the evidence?
faqs.answer: The peer-reviewed research library is linked to every claim on this site. Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go are each backed by validated clinical evidence.

faqs.question: What about HHVBP and Five-Star?
faqs.answer: Falls outcomes feed HHVBP and Five-Star measures directly. Protecting falls outcomes protects revenue.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your workflow.
blocks.bodyText: 20 minutes with a clinician who has run it in the field.
